---
title: |
  Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML<br>Unit 4: Probability Foundations
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML"
---

## 01. Title: Probability Foundations

- Unit role and dependency map.

## 02. Learning outcomes

- Core probabilistic language for ML.

## 03. Why probability is central in AI/ML

- Modeling uncertainty, not just averages.

## 04. Recap from Units 1–3

- LA + optimization connection points.

## 05. Random experiments and sample spaces

- Omega and events.

## 06. Events and sigma-algebras (light)

- What is measurable.

## 07. Probability axioms (Kolmogorov)

- Non-negativity, normalization, additivity.

## 08. Consequences of axioms

- Complement and union rules.

## 09. Counting refresher

- Permutations, combinations basics.

## 10. Probability via counting

- Uniform finite spaces.

## 11. Conditional probability

- P(A|B) intuition.

## 12. Chain rule

- Decomposition of joint probabilities.

## 13. Independence

- Pairwise vs mutual independence.

## 14. Common independence mistakes

- Correlation and causality confusion.

## 15. Law of total probability

- Partition-based reasoning.

## 16. Bayes theorem

- Prior, likelihood, posterior.

## 17. Base-rate effect

- Why priors matter in practice.

## 18. Medical-test toy example

- Posterior interpretation.

## 19. ML example: spam filtering

- Bayesian decision framing.

## 20. ML example: defect detection

- Rare-event posterior pitfalls.

## 21. Random variables

- Mapping outcomes to numbers.

## 22. PMF/PDF/CDF overview

- Discrete vs continuous views.

## 23. Expectation

- Average as linear operator.

## 24. Variance and standard deviation

- Dispersion quantification.

## 25. Covariance and correlation

- Linear dependence measures.

## 26. Joint and marginal distributions

- Summation/integration views.

## 27. Conditional distributions

- Updating knowledge with evidence.

## 28. Key distributions I

- Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson.

## 29. Key distributions II

- Uniform, Gaussian, Exponential.

## 30. Gaussian geometry intuition

- Mean and covariance roles.

## 31. Central Limit Theorem (conceptual)

- Why Gaussians appear often.

## 32. Law of Large Numbers (conceptual)

- Empirical convergence.

## 33. Monte Carlo intuition

- Simulation-based estimation.

## 34. Concentration intuition

- How uncertainty shrinks with n.

## 35. Probabilistic model viewpoint

- Data-generating assumptions.

## 36. Likelihood function

- Parameters as unknowns.

## 37. MAP vs MLE preview

- Bridge to inference unit.

## 38. Calibration concept

- Probabilities should match frequencies.

## 39. Miscalibration examples

- Over/under-confident models.

## 40. Uncertainty taxonomy

- Aleatoric vs epistemic preview.

## 41. Probability in loss design

- NLL and cross-entropy motivation.

## 42. Decision thresholds and risk

- Costs and class imbalance.

## 43. Leakage through probabilistic shortcuts

- Invalid confidence gains.

## 44. Common exam traps

- Conditioning direction errors.

## 45. Summary table

- Formula sheet scaffold.

## 46. Exercise 1 scaffold

- Conditional probability drill.

## 47. Exercise 2 scaffold

- Bayes + base-rate scenario.

## 48. Exercise 3 scaffold

- Distribution fit and interpretation.

## 49. Unit summary

- What students must retain.

## 50. References + transition to Unit 5

- Random variables and key distributions deep dive.

::: {#refs}
:::
